title: mentha × rotundifolia f. clandestina
intro: Mentha × rotundifolia f. clandestina, also known as wild mint, is a subspecies of mint native to Europe and Western Asia. This perennial herb is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Mentha × rotundifolia f. clandestina include relief from nausea, headaches, and menstrual cramps, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. It can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used in cooking and as a natural flavoring agent.
